title: React-backed kanbus_clock experiment
description: Build an experimental kanbus clock path that renders clock/info cards from Storybook React components and drives the Pixoo from that output.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Debugging React text blur: comparing working screen/font test pipeline (192 capture + nearest/binary) against new Storybook clock/info experiment. Next change will align experiment capture settings with known crisp path and add frame debug output.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applied crisp-capture alignment in react_storybook_clock_info: forcing 192x192 viewport + nearest downsample (matching screen/font tests). Added optional --info-threshold for hard text edge testing, default disabled. Running live device test now.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Found scaling bug source: Storybook stories are authored for 64x64 layout, so forcing viewport 192 shrank effective content to ~1/3 after downsample. Switched experiment capture to viewport_size=64 + device_scale_factor=3 + nearest downsample. Re-running on device now.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented Storybook readiness gating for React capture: preview now sets window.__pixooReady=false, waits for document.fonts.ready and Tiny5 load, then flips ready after two animation frames. Browser renderer already waits on __pixooReady, so captures should stop racing fallback fonts.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Removed fallback font families in Storybook components (TinyText/KanbusCard/LetterA now use Tiny5 only). Tightened Storybook ready contract: capture fails unless Tiny5 is verifiably loaded; browser renderer now surfaces __pixooReadyError instead of silently proceeding.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Switching to hard diagnostics per user request: capture LetterA story frame directly, measure lit-pixel bounding boxes in Python, and compare pre-upload frame geometry against expected Tiny5 shape. Next patch will be based on measured frame data.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Measured capture results confirm bug is in Storybook source frame, not upload: LetterA story renders only a 3x3 lit glyph (bbox=(0,1)-(2,3)) across capture modes. This reproduces user report exactly. Next step: replace React text rendering path with deterministic pixel-glyph renderer (same canonical masks used by working tinytext/font showcase pipeline), then retest.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Calibration result: after increasing Tiny5 CSS size from 5px->8px in React story components, LetterA capture now measures bbox 4x5 (was 3x3). This matches expected glyph height class (4/5). Running live React clock/info demo with this calibration for user confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing requested refactor: adding reusable KanbusHeader component and switching Kanbus info-card text stack to Bytesized font for higher density. Also wiring Bytesized font into Storybook via @font-face/static asset so React capture uses the intended font.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented reusable Storybook header component (KanbusHeader) and switched Kanbus card header/body text rendering to Bytesized. Added Bytesized @font-face asset in Storybook and strict readiness checks for Tiny5+Bytesized before capture. Demo restarted for device validation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying header spacing rules: fixed 3px gaps between [prefix][type][status], and replacing all internal whitespace in each token with explicit 2px pixel spacers. Restarting React demo after patch for visual confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added React Storybook parity target for original Python clock look: new story Pixoo/Clock -> PixooclockDefault with explicit geometry/colors (dot12, no second hand, larger top dot, tuned hand lengths/thicknesses). Updated react_storybook_clock_info default clock story to this parity story. Storybook build passes.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Adding Storybook comment-card matrix per request: task/story/bug/epic x {with_parent, without_parent}. Story names will be explicit and ready for visual tuning in Storybook/device loop.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added Storybook comment matrix stories for issue types and parent/no-parent variants: task/story/bug/epic x {with_parent, without_parent}. Preserved existing story id alias  -> task without parent so running demo links remain stable. Storybook build passes.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added Storybook comment matrix stories for issue types and parent/no-parent variants: task/story/bug/epic x {with_parent, without_parent}. Preserved existing story id alias CommentCard -> task without parent so running demo links remain stable. Storybook build passes.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Addressing two regressions: (1) lighten header background band shades across issue types for better visibility; (2) smooth transitions by decoupling Storybook clock capture from ScenePlayer render path (cached frame refresh loop), so transitions no longer wait on per-frame browser rendering.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing parent-row type tinting: add parentType prop to KanbusCard and color parent rows by parent kind (epic -> indigo band, initiative -> magenta band). Updating all with-parent stories to pass parentType and restarting demo for visual validation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing explicit fix: transition no-parent issue description rows from 7 to 10 lines for real-event cards.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updated transition no-parent description capacity to 10 lines and triggered a live transition for visual verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing requested layout change: transition cards with parent now allocate 7 lines to the issue description section (up from 4).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Root cause identified: transition card row height caused clipping, so requested line count was not fully visible. Applying row-height reduction so all configured lines render on-screen.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fixing regression: transition parent+issue layout should remain 3 parent rows + 7 issue rows with readable row height. Adjusting transition header/body sizing accordingly.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applying deterministic fix: transition-with-parent issue description now uses adaptive tighter wrap to fill 7 rows (when content permits) instead of stopping at ~4.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Root-cause write-up: repeated failures came from changing line limits without enforcing viewport fit, plus missing targeted tests. Implemented hard fill-to-viewport logic for transition cards and now adding assertions for 3+7 (parent) and 10 (no-parent).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting transition description height fix: enforce 7 (parent) / 10 (no-parent) visible lines via transition-only sizing + wrap width adjustment, with tests.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented transition-only wrap width (54px) for issue descriptions and header min height (font+2), fill-to-viewport rows for 7/10 line targets. Added targeted transition row-fill test. Note: existing palette/header background tests still failing (pre-existing).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Transition wrapping updated to tighten width iteratively (word-wrap only) for 7/10 lines; transition tests now assert viewport fill. Tests passing (34).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Switched all info screens to use issue titles (fallback to description if title missing). Transition wrapping now uses title-based wrap with 7/10 line targets. Tests passing (34).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing Kanbus card unification: comment layout as canonical for created/transition; titles in top blocks; comment bottom in sand, created/transition bottom in issue-type color; Storybook row counts.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented comment layout canonicalization: created/transition now match comment layout (2+2+5 or 2+7), top blocks use titles, transition/created bottom uses description in issue-type color, comment bottom uses sand. Tests passing (35).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment layout check: title in top blocks, comment in sand.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Changed created/transition to use issue titles for bottom text (no descriptions). Comment/transition/created now share the same layout with titles in top blocks; comment bottom stays sand. Tests passing (35).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added --no-repl and auto-disable when no TTY; EOFError in REPL now exits cleanly. This prevents background kanbus_clock from dying and allows continuous watch/clock.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Working on header status color: make transition + created issue status two notches lighter than current in header. Will locate React header token color for status and adjust for those card types, then validate via Storybook + device.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented header status lighten for transition/created cards in Storybook React: added per-token color support in KanbusHeader and set statusColor to a +2-notch lighten (20% mix to white) for created/transition only. Ready to validate on device.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing comment text +1 notch brightness in React KanbusCard (comment bottom text). Will adjust COMMENT_TEXT_COLOR upward and validate on device.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment text brightness bump applied: COMMENT_TEXT_COLOR -> #E8E0CB (one notch lighter). Ready for visual check.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Filed bug for non-Radix palette in React cards: PIXO-fa4c2e.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarting Storybook + kanbus_clock to pick up brighter comment text color change and validate status lighten.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Adjusting comment text color in Python kanbus_clock (InfoScene) from dark.sand8 to dark.sand9 for +1 notch brightness; updating tests and restarting demo.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Restarted Storybook + kanbus_clock after raising comment text to dark.sand9. Please re-check comment card brightness.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: kanbus_clock not running after restart; restarting now and retriggering a transition to verify clock returns.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: React clock stalled because Storybook server was down; restarting Storybook now so clock can resume updates.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness bumped to dark.sand9 (Python InfoScene). Restarted Storybook + kanbus_clock for live verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Bumping comment text to +2 notches (dark.sand10) in Python InfoScene; will restart kanbus_clock after change for visual check.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applied comment text +2 notch brightness (dark.sand10). Restarting kanbus_clock now after Playwright connection crash.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand10)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Bumping comment text two more notches to dark.sand12 (from sand10). Will restart kanbus_clock after change.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand12)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Adjusting comment text one notch dimmer to dark.sand11 and restarting kanbus_clock; will trigger a transition for visual check.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand11)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand11)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updating Python InfoScene header status color for transition/created: status token should match main body brightness. Will add header_status_color param and pass main_fg for created/transition.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Applied header_status_color override: transition/created status token now uses main_fg (bright) while id/type stay header_fg. Restarting kanbus_clock for visual confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand11)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Status token now uses main_fg (bright) on transition/created headers. Restarted kanbus_clock and triggered a transition + comment for verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Comment brightness check (dark.sand11)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Notification check

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Adding debug logs in auto notice consumer to confirm notices are dequeued/enqueued; will restart kanbus_clock and re-trigger events.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Triggered 3 Kanbus comment events (tskl-nlh.1). Watcher logs show 3 comment_added events and transitions enqueued; workspace-root kbs show still timing out and falling back to project-root. Clock session running with --debug.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting fix: restore push-left transitions for Kanbus clock notifications (currently popping). Will trace ScenePlayer enqueue path and transition specs in demos/kanbus_clock.py.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fixing pop-in transitions: React clock rendering is now cached-only on the render thread; heavy Storybook render moved entirely to background refresh + warmup to avoid blocking ScenePlayer frames during transitions. This should restore push-left animation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: kanbus clock test: comment to trigger notification
